mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.5% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.5% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives corporate earnings expectations upward and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth.
failure_condition: Correlation between month-over-month PAYEMS growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (Spearman rho < 0.15, p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, equity investors increase risk appetite, producing positive 20-day SPX returns through improved earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month in the same month
failure_condition: Joint positive signals in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential margin compression across the S&P 500 constituent base, producing negative 5-day forward returns as investors reduce equity exposure in anticipation of earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 2.5%) signals monetary tightening that increases real discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative 20-day SPX returns as investors rotate away from growth equities.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power, producing negative 60-day SPX returns as investors anticipate earnings compression and recession risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.0% and remains elevated for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining month-over-month signals weakening demand for capital goods and construction services, which cascades into reduced corporate investment and employment expectations, producing negative 20-day SPX returns as the market reprices growth downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals broad-based economic expansion with improving productivity and labor utilization, producing positive 60-day SPX returns as investors increase equity allocations in response to sustained growth momentum.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.3% and PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.1% in the same month
failure_condition: Joint positive signals in INDPRO and PAYEMS fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
